questions.question: Accountability is generally a major theme in all governance frameworks.
questions.explanation: Accountability is a major theme in all governance frameworks. This is stated clearly in the material.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 1, 1.1 - Understanding Corporate Governance

questions.question: The form of applied or professional ethics, that examines ethical principles and moral or ethical problems that can arise in a business environment is referred to as
questions.explanation: Business ethics (also called corporate ethics) examines ethical problems in business. This matches the definition given.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2, 2.1 - Business Ethics

questions.question: Relatively permanent and deeply held underlying beliefs and attitudes that help determine a person behavior is known as
questions.explanation: Values are relatively permanent and deeply held beliefs that guide behavior. This is the exact definition in the material.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2, 2.1 - Business Ethics

questions.question: The corporate governance theory which aims to ensure that managers pursue effectively shareholders best interest is ______ theory.
questions.explanation: Agency theory aims to ensure managers pursue shareholders' best interests. It uses performance pay and monitoring.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 4, 4.4 - Agency Theory

questions.question: Which corporate governance theory stipulate that Management will be opportunistic? ______ theory.
questions.explanation: Transaction cost theory states that managers will be opportunistic. They organise transactions for their own convenience.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 4, 4.5 - Transaction Cost Theory

questions.question: All the following are the main areas of the OECD Corporate Governance Principles except ______.
options: the responsibility of the chairman of the board
the role of stakeholders in corporate Governance
questions.explanation: The OECD areas include shareholder rights, equitable treatment, stakeholder role, disclosure, and board responsibilities. 'Responsibility of the chairman' is not one of the main areas.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 6, 6.2 - The OECD Principles of Corporate Governance

questions.question: A way in which companies take responsibility for the social and environmental impacts of their business operations is referred to as ______ responsibility.
questions.explanation: Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) is how companies take responsibility for their social and environmental impacts. This is the full term.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 1, 1.3 - Corporate Citizenship & Corporate Social Responsibility

questions.question: Which component of CSR expect an organization to do what is right, just and fair?
questions.explanation: Ethical responsibility means doing what is right, just, and fair. It goes beyond what the law requires.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 1, 1.3 - CSR has four components of responsibility

questions.question: The pyramid for CSR is in order of ______.
questions.explanation: The CSR pyramid goes from bottom to top: Economic, Legal, Ethical, Philanthropic. This is the correct order.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 1, 1.3 - Pyramid of Corporate Social Responsibility

questions.question: An organizations obligation to maximize positive stakeholders' benefits while minimizing effect of its action is termed as ______ responsibility.
questions.explanation: Corporate Social Responsibility is the obligation to maximize positive stakeholder benefits and minimize negative effects. This is the definition.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 2, 2.2 - Corporate Social Responsibility

questions.question: Equal opportunity statement is one of the basic elements of CSR
questions.explanation: Equal opportunities statements are listed as basic elements of CSR. They show commitment to fairness.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 2, 2.3 - Basic Elements of Corporate Social Responsibility

questions.question: Which of the following is one of the Corporate Responsibility stances?
questions.explanation: The four stances are Laissez-faire, Enlightened self-interest, Multiple stakeholder obligations, and Shaper of society. Laissez-faire is one of them.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 2, 2.4 - Corporate Social Responsibility Stances

questions.question: A strategy which a business follows where it is prepared to take full responsibility for its actions is known to be a ______ strategy.
questions.explanation: Proactive strategy means taking full responsibility before being forced. The company acts voluntarily and early.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 3, 3.1 - Strategies for Social Responsibility

questions.question: ABC limited involves in a strategy to minimize or avoid additional obligation arising from a particular problem, which strategy is ABC limited using?
questions.explanation: Defence strategy means minimising or avoiding additional obligations. The company tries to do the bare minimum.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 3, 3.1 - Strategies for Social Responsibility

questions.question: All the following are issues to consider in relation to sustainability and the triple bottom line except ______
questions.explanation: The three areas are environmental quality, social dimension, and economic issues. 'Environmental Issue' is too vague - the correct term is 'Environmental quality.'
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 4, 4.1 - Sustainability and Strategy

questions.question: Ohmae offers five reasons for globalization, which of the following is not one of the reasons?
questions.explanation: Ohmae's five Cs are Customer, Company, Competition, Currency, and Country. Co-workers is not one of them.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 6, 6.2 - Ohmae offers five reasons for globalisation

questions.question: The main aim of ______ is to develop a full marketing capability and offer products suited to local requirements.
questions.explanation: Insiderisation means setting up full functional organisations overseas. The aim is to offer products suited to local needs.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 6, 6.1 - International Strategy Choices

questions.question: In international business, the risk that arises from the possibility that the business idea itself might be flawed is termed as a / an ______ risk.
questions.explanation: Business risk arises when the business idea itself might be flawed. It exists in all markets but is bigger internationally.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 6, 6.1 - Market selection

questions.question: ______ occurs where a firm's goods are sold by other organizations who can offer greater market knowledge export.
questions.explanation: Indirect exporting happens when other organisations sell the firm's goods. These organisations have better market knowledge.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 6, 6.4 - Modes of entry to foreign markets

questions.question: The purpose of having ______ Directors is to provide unbiased and impartial perspective on issues to the board.
questions.explanation: Outside directors provide unbiased and impartial perspectives. They are not part of the management team.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 1, 1.3 - Outside directors
